title: Inflation in the US falls to 3.5% and markets react positively, although the Fed maintains its vigilance
summary: June CPI surprises on the downside and halts bets on rate hikes, but Fed Governor Warsh says the central bank 'will not tolerate' high inflation, which tempers optimism.

title: Oil spikes 22% in two weeks due to military escalation in the Persian Gulf
summary: The US launches airstrikes while Iran attacks tankers with cruise missiles, pushing Brent crude to $87 and straining global energy markets.

title: IBM plummets 25% on the stock market after cutting forecasts due to IT spending shifting towards artificial intelligence
summary: Enterprise clients redirect their IT budgets towards AI infrastructure, harming the company's traditional sales and dragging down the software sector as a whole.

title: Fear of an artificial intelligence bubble shakes the technology sector
summary: HSBC cuts Arm's rating, CoreWeave seeks coverage for chip prices, and fund manager Panza warns of excessive optimism, as investors debate whether it's time to buy or flee.

title: Wall Street banks record record profits in the first half of the year
summary: JP Morgan leads the results of the stock market 'Super Tuesday', although its CEO, Jamie Dimon, warns about geopolitical and inflation risks in a high-rate environment.

title: The Ibex resists pressure from oil and Treasury bills climb to 2.6%, their highest level since 2024
summary: Analysts recommend caution, highlight the potential of Indra after its fall and the healthcare sector, while short-term debt yields continue to attract investors.

title: Space sector stocks lose $89 billion in one day, but orders remain at record highs
summary: The market wonders whether the sector is a bubble or the future of transportation, while SpaceX captures the attention of retail and institutional investors.

title: The ECB selects 36 entities for the digital euro pilot and includes critics of the project
summary: The European central bank advances in the development of the digital euro, even recruiting detractors for the testing phase, in a process that could transform payments in the Eurozone.

title: Small-cap stocks and cybersecurity lead the gains on Wall Street
summary: Investors rotate into small-cap stocks and the cybersecurity sector surges, with CrowdStrike and Monolithic rising more than 10%, while Goldman Sachs and Nvidia also stand out.

title: Tokyo Stock Exchange rises sharply driven by the advance of semiconductors on Wall Street
summary: The Nikkei index extends its winning streak and surpasses 68,000 points thanks to optimism in the US technology sector and the weakness of the yen.

title: Japanese economy shows signs of weakness in machinery orders, while BOJ publishes unprecedented minutes
summary: Machinery orders fall 12.4% in May, although the government maintains its recovery assessment, and the Bank of Japan reveals the intense debates behind the adoption of negative rates in 2016.

title: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries surges on the stock market after announcing an alliance with Nvidia for AI data centers
summary: The strategic collaboration boosts the value of the Japanese company, which joins Nvidia's growing global artificial intelligence infrastructure.

title: Shohei Ohtani returns to the Dodgers lineup for the second half of the MLB season
summary: The Japanese phenomenon will play again on July 18, although his role as a pitcher remains undecided by the medical and technical staff.

title: China plans ambitious 60 trillion yuan consumption expansion plan for 2030
summary: The Chinese government prepares a massive stimulus to counter the economic slowdown and improve domestic consumption indicators amid deteriorating macroeconomic data.

title: Trump modifies his immigration policy and the Supreme Court requests more security in the face of increased threats
summary: The US president stops vehicle immigration checks after a fatal incident, while the high court judges ask for protection due to the increase in serious intimidation cases.

title: Japan debates reform of the Imperial House and advances in infrastructure while remembering its legends
summary: The Japanese parliament discusses the imperial succession law, the Shinkansen route to Osaka is confirmed, a victim's family requests a retrial, and tribute is paid to yokozuna Tamanoumi.

title: Lucid Motors shares plummet amid rumors of possible delisting or bankruptcy
summary: The US luxury electric vehicle manufacturer could opt for a buyout by a private investor or file for bankruptcy, according to market reports.

title: Evotec plummets on the stock market after drastically cutting its financial forecasts
summary: The German biotechnology company suffers a sharp stock market punishment caused by the reduction of its business expectations for the next fiscal year.

title: Boeing records its best first half in airplane deliveries since 2018
summary: The US aerospace manufacturer shows signs of recovery by increasing the number of aircraft delivered to its clients in the first six months of the year.

title: Elon Musk's artificial intelligence project generates pollution affecting African American communities
summary: An investigation reveals that the use of unauthorized electric generators for the xAI plant is disproportionately harming the black population in the area.
